meta.importedFrom: Unit 1 Passage 2 From mouse catcher to space tour guide...（互动课文教学动画）英语新教材外研版八年级下册.html

article.title: From mouse catcher to space tour guide...
article.summary: 从童年兴趣到职业想象，理解梦想是如何被慢慢打开的。

sentences.text: People's jobs have changed throughout history.
sentences.tr: 纵观历史，人们的工作一直在变化。
sentences.g: 现在完成时 + 时间状语
sentences.d: 'have changed'现在完成时表示从过去到现在的变化。'throughout history'表示贯穿整个历史。

sentences.text: If you lived in the mid-1300s, you might have been a mouse catcher.
sentences.tr: 如果你生活在14世纪中期，你可能会是一名捕鼠人。
sentences.g: 虚拟条件句（与过去事实相反）
sentences.d: 条件从句用过去时'lived'，主句用'might have been'，表示对过去情况的虚拟假设。

sentences.text: The job was well respected centuries ago, but few people do it today.
sentences.tr: 这份工作在几个世纪前很受尊重，但今天很少有人做这个工作。
sentences.g: 转折复合句 + 时间对比
sentences.d: 'but'连接转折关系，对比过去和现在的情况。'well respected'表示被动含义。

sentences.text: Now, technology is developing at an even faster speed.
sentences.tr: 现在，技术正以更快的速度发展。
sentences.g: 现在进行时 + 比较级
sentences.d: 'is developing'现在进行时强调正在进行的动作。'even faster'使用比较级强调速度更快。

sentences.text: Some traditional jobs are disappearing.
sentences.tr: 一些传统工作正在消失。
sentences.g: 现在进行时
sentences.d: 'are disappearing'现在进行时表示正在发生的变化趋势。

sentences.text: However, new ones are springing up to meet new needs.
sentences.tr: 然而，新的工作正在涌现以满足新的需求。
sentences.g: 转折句 + 目的状语
sentences.d: 'spring up'是短语动词，表示快速出现。'to meet'不定式作目的状语。

sentences.text: Cashiers in shops and supermarkets may not be necessary in the near future.
sentences.tr: 在不久的将来，商店和超市的收银员可能不再必要。
sentences.g: 情态动词 + 否定 + 时间状语
sentences.d: 'may not be'表示可能性的否定推测。'in the near future'表示不久的将来。

sentences.text: They may soon be replaced by machines.
sentences.tr: 他们可能很快会被机器取代。
sentences.g: 情态动词 + 被动语态
sentences.d: 'may be replaced'情态动词加被动语态，表示可能被取代。'by machines'表示动作执行者。

sentences.text: People can pay on their own in these places.
sentences.tr: 人们可以在这些地方自助付款。
sentences.g: 情态动词 + 动词原形
sentences.d: 'on their own'表示独立地、自己。'can'表示能力。

sentences.text: Autopilots were first used in planes over a century ago.
sentences.tr: 自动驾驶仪在一个多世纪前首次用于飞机。
sentences.g: 被动语态 + 时间状语
sentences.d: 'were used'被动语态表示被使用。'over a century ago'表示一百多年前。

sentences.text: With the development of new technology, perhaps there will be no human pilots in the future!
sentences.tr: 随着新技术的发展，也许未来将不会有人类飞行员！
sentences.g: with短语 + there be结构 + 将来时
sentences.d: 'with'表示伴随。'there will be'表示将来存在。'perhaps'表示可能性。

sentences.text: Space tourism started over 20 years ago.
sentences.tr: 太空旅游在20多年前就开始了。
sentences.g: 一般过去时 + 时间状语
sentences.d: 'started'一般过去时表示过去发生的动作。'over 20 years ago'表示20多年前。

sentences.text: In the future, travelling to space will surely be provided at a lower cost.
sentences.tr: 在未来，太空旅行肯定会以更低的成本提供。
sentences.g: 将来时被动语态 + 副词修饰
sentences.d: 'will be provided'将来时被动语态。'surely'副词表示肯定。'at a lower cost'表示以更低的成本。

sentences.text: Space tour guides will be needed to lead people to the moon and beyond!
sentences.tr: 将需要太空导游带领人们前往月球及更远的地方！
sentences.g: 将来时被动语态 + 目的状语
sentences.d: 'will be needed'将来时被动语态。'to lead'不定式作目的状语。'and beyond'表示更远的地方。

sentences.text: In the future, more people will work and play in virtual reality.
sentences.tr: 在未来，更多的人将在虚拟现实中工作和娱乐。
sentences.g: 将来时 + 并列谓语
sentences.d: 'will work and play'并列谓语表示两个动作。'in virtual reality'表示在虚拟现实中。

sentences.text: People will need cool and fashionable clothes, so VR fashion designers will be greatly needed!
sentences.tr: 人们将需要酷炫时尚的服装，所以VR时尚设计师将非常需要！
sentences.g: 因果复合句 + 被动语态
sentences.d: 'so'连接因果关系。'will be needed'将来时被动语态，'greatly'副词修饰。

sentences.text: The idea of flying in a plane without a human pilot is scary!
sentences.tr: 在没有人类飞行员的飞机上飞行的想法很可怕！
sentences.g: 主系表结构 + 介词短语作定语
sentences.d: 'of flying'介词短语作定语修饰idea。'without'表示没有。'is scary'主系表结构。

sentences.text: Is the technology safe enough?
sentences.tr: 这项技术足够安全吗？
sentences.g: 一般疑问句 + enough修饰形容词
sentences.d: 'enough'修饰形容词时后置。疑问句表示质疑。

sentences.text: Will pilots' jobs go up in smoke?
sentences.tr: 飞行员的工作会化为乌有吗？
sentences.g: 一般将来时疑问句 + 习语
sentences.d: 'go up in smoke'是习语，表示化为乌有、失败。所有格'pilots''表示飞行员的。

sentences.text: Um, sign me up for a space tour, please!
sentences.tr: 嗯，请给我报名参加太空旅行！
sentences.g: 祈使句 + 短语动词
sentences.d: 'sign up for'是短语动词，表示报名参加。'me'是宾语。'please'表示礼貌请求。

sentences.text: But what if I lose my job as a cashier?
sentences.tr: 但如果我失去收银员的工作怎么办？
sentences.g: what if句型 + 条件从句
sentences.d: 'what if'表示假设，相当于'如果...会怎样'。'as a cashier'表示作为收银员的身份。

sentences.text: How will I make enough money to pay for the tour?
sentences.tr: 我怎么赚足够的钱来支付旅行费用？
sentences.g: 特殊疑问句 + 目的状语
sentences.d: 'How will I'询问方式。'to pay for'不定式作目的状语。'enough'修饰money。

sentences.text: How fast the job market changes!
sentences.tr: 就业市场变化得多快啊！
sentences.g: 感叹句
sentences.d: 'How + 形容词/副词 + 主语 + 谓语'构成感叹句。'fast'副词修饰changes。

sentences.text: We can't just sit at our desks and expect things to stay the same.
sentences.tr: 我们不能只是坐在办公桌前，期待事情保持不变。
sentences.g: 否定句 + 并列谓语 + 复合宾语
sentences.d: 'sit'和'expect'是并列谓语。'expect + 宾语(things) + 不定式(to stay)'构成复合宾语结构，表示期待某事做某事。'stay the same'保持不变。

readingQuestions.stem: According to the passage, what does the phrase 'go up in smoke' in the comments section most likely mean?
options: C. Move to a higher position
readingQuestions.rationaleZh: 从上下文'Will pilots' jobs go up in smoke?'可以看出，这是在担心飞行员的工作会消失，因此'go up in smoke'是习语，表示化为乌有、消失，答案是B。

readingQuestions.stem: Which of the following jobs is mentioned as an example of a job that existed in the past but is rare today?
readingQuestions.rationaleZh: 文章第一段明确提到'If you lived in the mid-1300s, you might have been a mouse catcher. The job was well respected centuries ago, but few people do it today.'，因此答案是B。

readingQuestions.stem: What is the main reason given for why cashiers may not be necessary in the future?
options: C. Machines can replace them for self-payment
D. There are not enough cashiers
readingQuestions.answer: C. Machines can replace them for self-payment
readingQuestions.rationaleZh: 文章提到'They may soon be replaced by machines. People can pay on their own in these places.'，说明收银员可能被机器取代，人们可以自助付款，因此答案是C。

readingQuestions.stem: We can infer from the passage that the author's attitude towards future job changes is:
readingQuestions.rationaleZh: 文章客观地介绍了工作的变化趋势，既提到传统工作消失，也提到新工作涌现，没有明显的情感倾向，只是提供信息，因此作者的态度是中立和信息性的，答案是B。

readingQuestions.stem: What is the main purpose of this passage?
options: A. To warn people about job loss
C. To explain how jobs are changing due to technology
readingQuestions.answer: C. To explain how jobs are changing due to technology
readingQuestions.rationaleZh: 文章标题是'From Mouse Catcher to Space Tour Guide'，全文围绕技术发展如何导致工作变化展开，既讲述了消失的工作，也介绍了新兴的工作，因此主旨是解释技术如何改变工作，答案是C。

develop.usageZh: 及物动词：develop a habit (养成习惯)；develop a plan (制定计划)；不及物动词：The city developed rapidly. (城市迅速发展。)
develop.example: Children develop important social skills through play. (孩子们通过玩耍发展重要的社交技能。)

disappear.usageZh: 不及物动词：disappear from sight (从视线中消失)；disappear into the crowd (消失在人群中)
disappear.example: The sun disappeared behind the clouds. (太阳消失在云层后面。)
